headline: PhD Candidate at MILA / Polytechnique Montreal
bioMarkdown:
I work at the intersection of continual learning and neural network optimization. In continual learning, I’m interested in how models should adapt under distribution shift as new tasks or data arrive over time. In optimization, I focus on understanding the loss landscape better in order to guide learning toward minima that generalize better. I’m advised by [Prof. Sarath Chandar](https://sarathchandar.in).

Some highlights: [Manifold Metric](https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.15895) (CoLLAs 2025, Oral), [Lookbehind-SAM](https://arxiv.org/abs/2307.16704) (ICML 2024), and [Critical Momenta](https://arxiv.org/abs/2307.09638) (TMLR 2024). These projects owe a great deal to collaborators—especially [Aristide Baratin](https://arisbar.org/) (Samsung SAIT AI Lab Montreal) and [Razvan Pascanu](https://www.razp.info/) (Google DeepMind)—and to many other co-authors.

Before Montreal, I completed my MS at [IIT Madras](https://www.iitm.ac.in) with [Prof. Balaraman Ravindran](https://www.cse.iitm.ac.in/~ravi/) and [Prof. Sarath Chandar](https://sarathchandar.in) at [RBCDSAI](https://rbcdsai.iitm.ac.in). There I worked on [TAG](https://arxiv.org/abs/2105.05155) (CoLLAs 2022) and [Causal Fairness](https://arxiv.org/abs/2111.14348) (ACML 2021).
blogTagline: Book notes, research reflections, and the occasional deep dive.
